back_translation: Then Bildad the Shuhite replied:
'How long will you say such things? The words of your mouth are a mighty wind.
Does God pervert justice? Does the Almighty pervert what is right?
If your children sinned against Him, He gave them over to the consequence of their transgression.
But if you would seek God and plead for mercy from the Almighty,
if you are pure and upright, surely now He will rouse Himself on your behalf and restore your righteous dwelling.
Though your beginning was small, your latter end will greatly flourish.
Please inquire of the former generation, and consider what their fathers discovered.
For we were born yesterday and know nothing; our days on earth are but a shadow.
Will they not teach you and tell you, and bring forth words from their understanding?
Can papyrus grow where there is no marsh? Can reeds flourish without water?
While still in bud and uncut, it withers faster than any grass.
Such is the way of all who forget God, and the hope of the godless perishes.
His confidence is fragile, and his trust is like a spider's web.
He leans on his house, but it does not stand; he holds fast to it, but it does not endure.
He is like a well-watered plant in the sunshine, spreading its shoots over the garden.
Its roots twine around a heap of stones and take hold among the rocks.
But when it is uprooted from its place, that place disowns it, saying, 'I never saw you.'
Behold, this is the joy of its way; and from the dust others spring up.
Behold, God will not reject the blameless, nor will He strengthen the hand of evildoers.
He will yet fill your mouth with laughter, and your lips with shouts of joy.
Those who hate you will be clothed in shame, and the tent of the wicked will be no more.'
